levels.title: Foundations
levels.subtitle: The modern web platform stack
levels.description: Build the mental model for how modern web applications actually run. Master the browser rendering pipeline, frontend frameworks, build systems, container and sandbox technologies, and the networking stack from DNS to edge. These are the primitives every platform conversation downstream assumes.

levels.title: The AI Code-Gen Landscape
levels.subtitle: From demo magic to product category
levels.description: Orient yourself in the prompt-to-code market. Understand the competitive architecture of v0, Lovable, Bolt, and Replit Agent; the prototype-to-production gap; how developers, non-technical builders, and AI agents each break the platform differently; and where Figma Make sits in the ecosystem.

levels.title: Code Execution & Runtimes
levels.subtitle: The hardest invisible problem in the stack
levels.description: The runtime is the foundational platform decision. Master the tradeoffs between server-side containers, browser-based execution (WebContainers), serverless and edge runtimes, and sandboxing mechanisms. Learn why cold starts, warm pools, and latency budgets determine what feels instant.

levels.title: Preview Environments & Deployment
levels.subtitle: From ephemeral sandbox to production URL
levels.description: Preview environments are where generated code meets reality. Master tenant isolation at scale, hot module replacement, dependency management in ephemeral environments, the preview-to-production pipeline, custom domains and edge delivery, and rollback as a safety primitive.

levels.title: Agent Infrastructure
levels.subtitle: The substrate that lets AI actually write code
levels.description: Coding agents are not chatbots with code output. Master the Observe-Think-Act-Evaluate loop, tool use and function calling, context management, multi-step reasoning, error recovery, and the build-fork-integrate decision. Extend into agent memory, multi-agent orchestration, and prompt architecture as a discipline.

levels.title: Eval Systems & Quality Gates
levels.subtitle: How to know the AI got better or worse before users find out
levels.description: Code-gen evaluation is structurally harder than traditional software testing. Master execution-based evaluation, visual regression, regression detection, LLM-as-judge with human calibration, eval infrastructure as a product, drift detection, and pre-deployment gates that catch what eval suites miss.

levels.title: Observability & Platform Health
levels.subtitle: The telemetry that makes AI platforms operable
levels.description: AI platforms degrade silently. Master observability for probabilistic systems — the three pillars (logs, metrics, traces), user-facing observability, platform-level monitoring with SLOs, cost observability and showback, and incident response patterns unique to AI systems.

levels.title: Extensibility & Platform Strategy
levels.subtitle: Building the platform other teams build on
levels.description: Platforms become durable through extensibility and strategic sequencing. Master APIs, SDKs, and plugin systems; the platform-product boundary; cross-product leverage; velocity versus platform health; GA readiness; multi-quarter roadmapping; and B2A API design for a world where AI agents are the primary consumers.
